meta.tagline_nl: Van chaos naar controle. Van onderbuik naar inzicht. Van start-up naar serieuze scale-up.
meta.backers: Duke Strategies (Ingo Heijnen, Ariën Stuijt — silent partners)

decisions.logo: L3 — Three Steps Up (abstract mark, three rising bars)
decisions.tagline_en: TG1 — Finance that scales with you.

brand.essence: Senior CFO leadership that turns gut-feel finance into the structure a scale-up actually needs.
brand.positioning: Start to Scale Financial Management is the senior finance partner that takes a growing SME from the kladje-on-the-back-of-the-envelope to a fully professional finance function — diagnosis, roadmap, hands-on implementation, and ongoing operation. Wilgert Hart brings nearly 30 years of CFO experience at WPP, EMI and Warner Music Benelux, backed by a network of finance professionals.
brand.audience: Owners and boards of NL-based start-ups making the scale-up transition (€1M–€20M revenue, growing fast, finance still informal).
brand.toneOfVoice: Plain Dutch. No jargon, no false urgency. Speaks like a senior CFO at a board table: direct, honest about what's broken, confident about how to fix it.

logo._note: Wordmark PNGs render via rsvg-convert with system font fallback (Georgia for Newsreader, system-ui for Inter). Convert wordmark SVG text to outlined paths via fontTools before final production print/PDF for exact Newsreader fidelity.

themes.name: Architectural Minimalism
themes.rationale: Clean lines, controlled light, sense of order — visual proxy for the structured finance function S2S installs.

themes.name: Data Clarity
themes.rationale: Charts, tables, dashboards rendered as quiet objects — turning raw data into decision-grade insight.

catalog.description: Quiet interior — diagonal light, single chair
Line chart on laptop — restrained editorial

images._note: 8 sourced photos × 6 variants each (source, 16x9, 2x1, 1x1, text-safe, branded) = 48 files. Full per-file map and credits in images/manifest.json.

motif.name: Three Steps
motif.rationale: Three ascending bars encode the brand's core promise: chaos → control → scale-up. Geometric, calm, repeatable at any scale.

rules.accentRule: Tallest bar gets a 12-unit amber cap signalling the scale-up outcome

pptxAssets._note: 19 overlay variants (navy-55, navy-35 for all 8 photos; bw-amber for cover/closing roles). Full list in pptx-assets/manifest.json.

archetype.rationale: Primary Sage: Wilgert Hart's 30-year CFO background is expressed as structured, diagnostic wisdom — turning gut-feel finance into professional rigour. Caregiver is secondary and structural: the brand installs a finance function and stays on to operate it, a hands-on support role grounded in structure rather than emotional warmth. Confirmed by Wilgert 2026-06-01 (charter.json meta.archetype + BUILD_LOG Phase 0 decision).

identity.essence: Senior CFO leadership that turns gut-feel finance into the structure a scale-up actually needs.
identity.positioning: Start to Scale Financial Management is the senior finance partner that takes a growing SME from kladje-on-the-back-of-the-envelope to a fully professional finance function — diagnosis, roadmap, hands-on implementation, and ongoing operation. Wilgert Hart brings nearly 30 years of CFO experience at WPP, EMI and Warner Music Benelux, backed by a network of finance professionals.

principles: Amber is a signal, not decoration — one amber element per surface (logo cap, CTA button, or data accent), never both a button and a link amber on the same page.
Body text is navy, not black — warmth and authority together; pure black reads cold and impersonal for a finance partner.
Backgrounds default to warm Paper (#FAF8F3), not pure white — paper reads as a professional partner, white reads as a software product.

The NL three-line hero tagline ('Van chaos naar controle. Van onderbuik naar inzicht. Van start-up naar serieuze scale-up.') is the primary headline on every deliverable cover; its staircase rhythm mirrors the Three Steps motif.
The Three Steps motif is a signature, not a pattern — used once per visible surface, never tiled at full opacity outside the background pattern tile (≤8% opacity).
Board-table voice: name what is broken, then say what fixes it. No jargon, no false urgency, no brochure register.
Images always carry a 55% navy tint overlay on hero/cover surfaces; the white logo variant is used on all image surfaces.
signatureElements: Three ascending bars (ratio 1:1.8:2.6, bar width 12, gap 6) with a single amber cap on the tallest bar — the only place amber appears in the mark.
Warm Paper background (#FAF8F3) as default surface, not pure white — distinguishes the advisory register from a software product.
Navy-90 / amber-10 horizontal divider (thin 1.5px line with amber terminal at right) — encodes the 'ascend' direction, structural punctuation between sections.
NL three-line tagline staircase as typographic hero — structurally echoes the ascending bar motif and is never translated literally to English.
Newsreader Medium (contemporary literary serif, -0.02em tracking) for all display and heading — carries the considered, sage register without classical stiffness.
Inter overline caps at 0.18em tracking, 11px — labels sections, wordmark subtitle 'FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT', badges; same tracking as the logo caption.
JetBrains Mono reserved exclusively for numbers, tables, and data-adjacent contexts — signals that figures are decision-grade, not decoration.
Dual image themes of equal weight: Architectural Minimalism (structural still-life) and Data Clarity (charts/dashboards as quiet artefacts) — no people, no handshakes, no mountains.
antiPatterns: Multiple amber elements on a single surface (amber is a single-use signal per page).
Pure black (#000000) or pure white (#FFFFFF) as primary background or body text — both break the warm advisory register.
Gradients on body sections (gradientDirection is 'none' by design; gradient only appears in nav-amber-diag pptx asset).
Tilted, rotated, or recoloured Three Steps motif — must be upright navy bars with amber cap, no exceptions.
Fourth bar or dropped bar in the motif — the 1:1.8:2.6 progression is load-bearing meaning.
Mountains, Alpine, Dolomite, or Dutch-landscape stock photography (explicitly flagged by Wilgert Hart).
Handshake or suits-in-meeting stock photography — contra the architectural and data-clarity direction.
Mixed NL+EN within the same sentence in copy.
Buzzwords: 'synergy', 'next-level', 'agile', or any phrase that sounds like a brochure.
Pattern tile at full opacity on reading surfaces — restricted to 6–8% opacity on slide/section backgrounds only.
requiredMotifs: Three Steps ascending bars on all deliverable covers (small, left-aligned over section title in decks; as hero accent below the NL tagline on web).
Navy-90 / amber-10 thin-line divider as section rule on web and documents.
Warm Paper (#FAF8F3) as the default background surface on all light-mode deliverables.
White wordmark variant on all navy or image-overlay surfaces.
55% navy tint overlay on all hero and cover photograph treatments.
